description: A designed DHS graphic styled as a 'headline wall' / press-clipping collage on a dark navy-blue textured background that resembles faded newspaper print. A red banner across the top carries bold white all-caps text reading 'MAKING AMERICA SAFE AGAIN'. Below it, five torn-paper / ripped-newsprint cards are arranged in a staggered grid, each reproducing a mainstream news outlet logo above a quoted headline about Secretary Kristi Noem's visit to El Salvador's CECOT mega-prison. The cards read: (1) AXIOS - 'Noem warns immigrants during tour of El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans held'; (2) AP - 'Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held'; (3) Washington Examiner - 'Noem sends warning to illegal immigrants from El Salvador prison with Tren de Aragua members'; (4) FOX NEWS - 'Noem comes face-to-face with alleged Tren de Aragua members during tour of notorious El Salvador prison'; (5) The Washington Post - 'Noem threatens to send more immigrants to El Salvador prison'. The official circular DHS seal is centered at the bottom. The propaganda technique is the 'media validation' or earned-media montage: by stacking logos of ideologically varied outlets (left-leaning WaPo/Axios alongside Fox), the graphic frames the deportation/CECOT messaging as universally newsworthy and credible, and projects toughness and deterrence. Target audience: the general public and the administration's base, signaling that the crackdown is making national headlines.
notable_text: MAKING AMERICA SAFE AGAIN; 'Noem threatens to send more immigrants to El Salvador prison'; 'Noem comes face-to-face with alleged Tren de Aragua members'
